cedi: noun. The currency of Ghana, divided into 100 pesewas and represented by ₵.
cod: noun. Any of various types of sea fish or the meat from said fish.
code: noun. A short textual designation, often with little relation to the item it represents.
codec: noun. A device or program capable of performing transformations on a data stream or signal.
coin: noun. A piece of currency, usually metallic and in the shape of a disc, but sometimes polygonal, or with a hole in the middle.
coincide: verb. To occupy exactly the same space.
coined: verb. simple past and past participle of coin
con: verb. To study or examine carefully, especially in order to gain knowledge of; to learn, or learn by heart.
cone: noun. A surface of revolution formed by rotating a segment of a line around another line that intersects the first line.
coned: adjective. segregated or delineated by traffic cones
den: noun. A small cavern or hollow place in the side of a hill, or among rocks; especially, a cave used by a wild animal for shelter or
dice: noun. Gaming with one or more dice.
die: verb. To stop living; to become dead; to undergo death. followed by of as an indication of direct cause; general use:
din: noun. A loud noise; a cacophony or loud commotion.
dine: verb. To eat; to eat dinner or supper.
doe: noun. A female deer; also used of similar animals such as antelope (less commonly a goat, as nanny is also used).
don: noun. A university professor, particularly one at Oxford or Cambridge.
done: adjective. Having completed or finished an activity.
end: noun. The terminal point of something in space or time.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
icon: noun. An image, symbol, picture, portrait, or other representation, usually as an object of religious devotion.
iconic: adjective. Relating to, or having the characteristics of, an icon.
indie: adjective. Independent; from outside the mainstream.
iodic: adjective. of, or relating to iodine or its compounds, especially those in which it has a valency of five
iodine: noun. A chemical element (symbol: I) with an atomic number of 53; one of the halogens.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
ionic: adjective. of, relating to, or containing ions
nod: verb. To incline the head up and down, as to indicate agreement.
node: noun. A knot, knob, protuberance or swelling.
ode: noun. A short poetical composition proper to be set to music or sung; a lyric poem; especially, now, a poem characterized by sustained
odic: adjective. Of or pertaining to odes.
once: adverb. One and only one time.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
ceo: noun. Alternative letter-case form of CEO.
cid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chimariko.
dei: interjection. Alternative spelling of dey (“informal term of address used when trying to get someone's attention.”).
deni: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to 0.01 Macedonian denar.
dinic: adjective. Relating to vertigo or dizziness.
eco: adjective. Clipping of ecological: environmentally friendly or sensitive.
ide: noun. A freshwater fish of the family Cyprinidae, found across northern Europe and Asia, especially Leuciscus idus.
incide: verb. To separate and remove (something); to cut.
nco: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sibe.
ned: noun. A person, usually a youth, of low social standing and education, a violent disposition and with a particular style of dress
nid: noun. Alternative form of nide (“nest of pheasants”).
oie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Okolie.
oni: noun. A Japanese evil spirit or demon.
